Because I know there's a lot of use cases on the agency side, obviously, and that's who you're trying to get to buy.
But yeah, are there also opportunities on the beneficiaries of the agency having that platform?

I think, I wouldn't say no, because I do think there's always, you know, if they're saying, like, if the families are aware of the benefits of binti and they're bringing up to their agency, or if they have a positive experience with it, and like for upsells and stuff, I, so I don't, I would.
I say no.
I do think, like, our target audience when we say, for, like, PR efforts is, like, investors.
So, like, yeah, potential, like, tech investors, government officials, and, like, our, the agency workers.
So I think from, like, in general, I do think that those are kind of our key target audiences.
And obviously, it's, like, great if our families love us as well.
But I think that our focus is more on, like, yeah, we've been focused on, like, investors and potential customers.
And think for this, probably lean on customer side.
That makes sense because the families, like, they just get the benefit of a better experience when they're wanting to do one of these things.
And they don't really even know that Vinti is, like, a part of that process.
They were just, like, more, wow, this agency did a great job of, like, helping me understand, like, you know, what the options are and following up.
And your platform helps with that.
Yeah, and they do go, like, they're on the Vinti, it's, like, Vinti website.
And branding when they're going through the process, they definitely know it.
And I wouldn't say it's not helpful, but my guess is like we would heavily index on the actual customers.
Yeah, the question comes from making sure there's like enough content opportunities for us with different audiences that would benefit.
And it definitely sounds like there is if you factor in the inventors, investors, the government officials and agencies.
So, yeah.
And I think for SEO, I mean, maybe some investors, I do think the focus would be on customers for this.
So it would be like the, you know, it could be like governor level people, like government officials, even maybe federal level people.
And then like state and county leaders and then the specific agency leaders and then all the way down to like the specific social workers.
Awesome.
And I just thought of one more thing.
mean, if you have like 30 seconds, who are like y'all's top competitors that you would be trying to steal traffic from?
Because.
Because that can be something I do some analysis around, but ahead of the next conversation to actually, like, quantify the search opportunity.
Yeah.
So, honestly, our biggest competitors are, I would break up, like, into two.
I mean, there's definitely the Deloitte's and the Accenture's of the world are our biggest competitors because they build their own bespoke systems and they come in on these big consulting projects.
They often fail, they go over budget, go over timelines, etc.
But, like, they are definitely some of our competitors.
And then there is also...
Some, like, legacy options, you know?
Yeah, there's, like, Cardinality and Redmayne who do licensing for government.
So, they also are some of our competitors.
But I would say, yeah, like, Accenture, Deloitte, Cardinality, Redmayne.
And then, like, the Accenture's and Deloitte's of the world will go and build it on a Salesforce.
But I think it's, like, the...
Accenture, Deloitte, that is kind of the main top of my head one.
Okay, interesting.
So outside of the consulting firms, like in your category, like a real opportunity to like basically dominate the voice around like the content that is connected to these services.
Like there's no other competitor that's really like doing the full scope of what you do or like doing it as their main offering.
Yeah, no one is doing exactly what Binti is doing.
They're like Redmayne and Cardinality are like licensing.
So they help with licensing, but not specifically child welfare licensing, but they do.
They are definitely competitors.
Okay, awesome.
Awesome.
Well, great to meet you and looking forward to dive in deeper after I do a little more analysis on my side.
Awesome.
Cool.
All right.
And sorry, what time did we say?
Or yeah, if you can send that time today, that'd be good because Felicia's calendar books up quickly.
So I would just one to two right now is still available for Thursday, but perfect, you'll see.
in two minutes.
Amazing.
Okay.
Thank you so much.
Bye.
All right.
Talk soon.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introductory call to explore a content marketing partnership between Binti and GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=193.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Binti's Strategic Shift:** After achieving cashflow positivity, Binti is shifting from a pure enterprise sales model to investing in marketing to accelerate growth.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1238.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Model:** GrowthX provides a managed service to build a content engine that answers all buyer questions, optimizing for both human search and AI models.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=588.0)
  - [**Binti's Opportunity:** With a small site (49 pages) and competitors focused on consulting (Accenture, Deloitte) or narrow licensing (Cardinality, Redmayne), Binti has a clear path to become the dominant voice in child welfare tech.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1012.0)
  - [**Next Step:** A proposal review is scheduled for Thursday, Feb 12th, at 1 PM PT.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1891.0)

## Topics

### Binti's Strategic Context

  - [**Mission:** Improve the child welfare system via SaaS, helping every child find a family.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=206.0)
  - [**Product:** A suite of vertical SaaS solutions for child welfare teams (e.g., family matching, prevention).](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=244.0)
  - [**Market Position:**](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=234.0)
      - [Serves 550+ agencies in 37 states (49% market share).](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=224.0)
      - [Post-Series B, 90-person team.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=280.0)
  - [**Growth Strategy Shift:**](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1238.0)
      - [**Past:** Relied on enterprise sales, GovAffairs, and conferences.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=285.0)
      - [**Present:** Now cashflow positive, Binti is reinvesting in growth, doubling its sales team and hiring BDRs.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1320.0)
      - [**Rationale:** Early BDR success proves that driving more inbound traffic is a viable growth lever.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=300.0)

### GrowthX's Content Engine Model

  - [**Core Philosophy:** Build a content engine that becomes the definitive resource for a market, creating compounding returns.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)
  - [**Process:**](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=738.0)
    1.  [**Strategy Sprint (Phase 1):** Define audience, goals, and content strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0)
    2.  [**Ongoing Execution (Phase 2):** GrowthX acts as a fractional team, publishing daily (new or refreshed content) to build "surface area."](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=762.0)
  - [**Key Differentiator:** Optimizes for three audiences: human search, AI agents, and AI training bots.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=588.0)
  - [**Timeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=956.0)
      - [**Week 1:** V1 context artifacts](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=956.0)
      - [**Weeks 2–3:** Content strategy & roadmap](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=956.0)
      - [**Week 4:** First articles published](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=956.0)
      - [**End of 8 weeks:** 10–20+ articles published](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=956.0)
  - [**ROI:** A long-term play; strong signal typically appears in 6 months.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1142.0)

### Binti's Content Opportunity

  - [**Current State:** Binti's site has 49 pages, with only \~11 blog posts and minimal organic traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1012.0)
  - [**Target Audiences:**](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1936.0)
      - [**Primary:** State & county officials, agency leaders, social workers.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=2065.0)
      - [**Secondary:** Investors, government officials.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1980.0)
      - [**Not Primary:** Foster/adoptive families (they are users, not direct buyers).](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1936.0)
  - [**Competitive Landscape:**](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=2092.0)
      - [**Accenture & Deloitte:** Build bespoke, often over-budget systems.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=2092.0)
      - [**Cardinality & Redmayne:** Offer narrow licensing software, not child welfare-specific.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=2092.0)
  - [**Conclusion:** The market is wide open for Binti to become the dominant content authority.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=2166.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Send proposal and meeting invite for the follow-up.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1891.0)
  - [**Felicia & Rima:** Review the proposal.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1710.0)
  - [**All:** Meet to review the proposal.](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1891.0)
      - [**When:** Thursday, Feb 12th, at 1:00 PM PT](https://fathom.video/share/SJVmkqmQTc46pgBg9kyspcWnm8Qh1e6j?tab=summary&timestamp=1891.0)

action_items.description: Prepare proposal for Binti: scope, pricing, timeline
Send calendar invite to Felicia & Rima for Thu 1–2 pm PT follow-up
Analyze search opportunity for Binti vs Accenture, Deloitte, Cardinality, Redmayne
